By adding an explicit type annotation of `PerformanceData`, the compiler can correctly identify the variable's shape and allow access to its properties, resolving the errors.

payload.text: Let's be sure to add the appropriate settings too in system config please.

**Considering Feature Implementation**

I'm focusing on the user's request for a new feature, which seems centered around "search grounding" facilitated by the Gemini API. The prompt, while somewhat unclear, highlights the need for corresponding settings in the system configuration, particularly in the context of separating simulated and live data. This is my main point of focus right now.


**Defining Feature Specs**

I've moved towards defining specific requirements for this new feature. It revolves around integrating Google Search grounding into the Gemini API calls. I plan to add a configurable toggle switch in the system configuration to enable or disable this functionality. When enabled, the AI's Cognitive Feed will be grounded with real-time web data, displaying sources as per Gemini API guidelines. This represents a significant enhancement, and a specification is needed.


**Refining Functionality Details**

I'm now diving deeper into how the "Google Search Grounding" feature will work. The user's goal seems to be making the app's AI more "real". My current thinking is centered around the implementation details. I intend to integrate Google Search grounding into Gemini API calls. In the SYSTEM CONFIGURATION modal, under the // DATA SOURCE // section, I'll add a toggle labeled "Enable Google Search Grounding". UserPrefsService will manage the state of this toggle. I have started considering the changes needed in GeminiService.


**Refining Detailed Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the specific implementation steps. My focus is on integrating Google Search grounding with Gemini and ensuring a seamless user experience. I've broken down the tasks into system config updates, service modifications, and UI adjustments. Key changes include adding a `useSearchGrounding` signal in `UserPrefsService`, modifying the `GeminiService` to incorporate Google Search tools and handling source data, and updating the `IntelPanelComponent` to display source links. I'm also ensuring the correct typing of the source data.


**Defining Implementation Steps**

I've outlined a comprehensive plan for integrating "Google Search Grounding." My thinking has progressed to detailed specifications. I'll modify `UserPrefsService` to manage the grounding toggle, update `GeminiService` to utilize the Google Search tool and include source data, and adjust `IntelPanelComponent` to display source links. I'm also ensuring appropriate type definitions for the returned data and I have specified the file modification plan.


**Defining Implementation Specs**

I've clarified the implementation steps for "Google Search Grounding." I plan to add a configurable toggle in the system configuration within the "DATA SOURCE" section and manage it with the `UserPrefsService`. I'll update the `GeminiService` to use Google Search tools, adjust prompts for grounding, and return narrative alongside source data. Finally, I'll update the `IntelPanelComponent` to display sources as clickable links. I've defined the file modification plan, ensuring data type safety.


**Finalizing the Implementation**

I've completed the implementation of the Google Search Grounding feature. I've successfully integrated Google Search into the Gemini API calls. I've added the new toggle switch in the system config, managing its state with `UserPrefsService`. The `GeminiService` now incorporates Google Search tools and returns narrative alongside source data. Finally, I've updated the `IntelPanelComponent` to display sources as clickable links and ensured type safety.


**Implementing Search Grounding**

I'm now integrating Google Search grounding with the Gemini API to enhance narrative generation. My focus is on updating the user preferences service with a toggle for this feature and modifying the Gemini service to handle the integration. I'll need to define new types for the returned source data, update the intel panel component to display grounded narratives and sources as clickable links, and add the configuration toggle. This will require file modifications and a structured implementation plan.

Of course. Integrating real-time, grounded intelligence is a critical step in transforming Project Cygnus from a prototype into a production-grade terminal. Your "Y-Junction" analogy was a great foundation, and now we'll enhance the "live" branch of that junction with real-world data from Google Search.

I've implemented a new feature that allows the AI to ground its market narratives in real-time web data. This ensures the insights are current, relevant, and trustworthy.

Here is a summary of the key enhancements:

1.  **Google Search Grounding:** The AI cognitive feed can now be empowered with Google Search. When activated, it will pull in the latest news and data to inform its analysis, providing insights that are reflective of the current moment.

2.  **Transparent Sourcing:** In line with best practices for AI, whenever the system uses web data, it will now transparently cite its sources directly in the Intel Panel. You can click these links to view the original articles and data points, ensuring full auditability.

3.  **System Configuration:** I've added a new toggle in the "System Config" panel under the "Data Source" section. This gives you complete control over when to enable or disable search grounding, allowing you to switch between the model's internal knowledge and real-time web intelligence on the fly.

These changes significantly increase the power and reliability of the AI-driven insights, making the terminal a much more effective tool for real-world analysis.

Here are the code changes to implement this feature:

entries.description: Added the `GroundingChunk` interface to provide type safety for the source data returned by the Gemini API when Google Search grounding is enabled. This ensures that the application correctly handles and displays web sources.

Upgraded `generateMarketNarrative` to support Google Search grounding. The method now accepts a `useGrounding` flag, which adds the `googleSearch` tool to the Gemini API request. The return type has been updated to include both the narrative text and an array of `GroundingChunk` sources, ensuring that the UI can display cited information.

Enhanced the Intel Panel to support and display grounded search results. It now maintains a `sources` signal and calls the Gemini service with the user's preference for search grounding. The component template has been updated to render the list of web sources with clickable links below the AI narrative whenever they are available.

Added a toggle switch to the "System Config" modal for enabling or disabling Google Search grounding. This new control, located under the "Data Source" section, allows users to easily switch the AI's ability to access real-time web data for its market analysis.

entries.description: Added a new `useSearchGrounding` signal and a `toggleSearchGrounding` method to the `UserPrefsService`. This allows the application to manage the state for enabling or disabling Google Search grounding for AI-powered features, providing users with control over real-time data access.

entries.description: Fixes a type error in `calculateOutcome` where an empty object `{}` was likely being used as a default value for a variable of type `PerformanceData`. The fix replaces the invalid default with a call to `this.getInitialData()`, which returns a correctly structured object. The code is also improved by removing the now-redundant explicit type annotation and updating the comment to clarify the fix.

payload.text: Oh, this is a great idea! But let's actually make it a new algorithm instead!

**Developing Algorithm Idea**

I'm now focusing on developing an algorithm triggered by web search results, building upon the initial Google Search Grounding concept. The user wants a specific, practical algorithm for an app, not just narrative support. This is a brilliant step toward a more dynamic and responsive app experience.


**Defining Algorithm Features**

I've named the algorithm `NEWS_SPIKE`, and I'm planning a news-related icon for it. Its purpose is to spot sudden news mentions of tokens in the user's watchlist, which might signal volatility. The logic is now taking shape, focusing on periodic Google searches for each watchlist token using a query like "[Token Name] crypto price news." I'll integrate this with the `IntelPanel` refresh for on-demand use, using Google Search to detect recent, relevant news.


**Designing Algorithm Implementation**

I'm now detailing the implementation. The algorithm, which I've named `NEWS_SPIKE`, will be triggered periodically or manually via the `IntelPanel` refresh. I'm focusing on its logic: conducting Google searches for each watchlist token using a structured query like "[Token Name] crypto price news." A signal triggers if recent, relevant news is found. I will start by adding `NEWS_SPIKE` to the `SignalType` enum, and then creating a `checkForNewsSpike()` method in `gemini.service.ts` to build queries and parse responses.


**Refining Algorithm Design**

I'm now detailing the implementation. The `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm will be triggered periodically, or manually via the IntelPanel refresh, focusing on its logic. It will conduct Google searches for each watchlist token, using a structured query like "[Token Name] crypto price news." A signal triggers if recent, relevant news is found, focusing on how this will work with the Gemini API to get the results. The structure of the `Signal` needs modification, using two new methods and refactoring existing ones. The architecture will now include `syncSignals` and `asyncSignals`.


**Specifying Algorithm Requirements**

I've specified the requirements for the new `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm. I've defined its name, icon (which needs creating), purpose – detecting news mentions possibly signaling volatility – and logic. I've decided it shouldn't run on every tick, and the `IntelPanel` refresh will trigger it. I've designed the Google search query for news and its criteria for relevancy, and how I'll include the signal message and URL.



**Implementing News Spike Detection**

I'm now deep in implementing the `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm. I've defined its name, purpose, and logic, focusing on triggering alerts when news mentions a watchlist token. I'm building a system to integrate Gemini's Google Search tool to find recent and relevant news for each token. I'm modifying signal generation to handle async calls, which means a big refactor. I'm also now adding components and setting up async signals using `IntelPanel` to trigger it. I'm adding a new icon, writing docs, and tracking performance, with the biggest changes on the token state service to support async calls.


**Refining Algorithm Design**

I'm now diving into the implementation. I'm focusing on incorporating the Gemini API's `GoogleSearch` tool to hunt for relevant news, specifically for the `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm. The `checkForNewsSpike` method will require constructing structured queries and handling responses. I'm now modifying the `Signal` structure to handle async calls, which necessitates creating two new methods and refactoring existing ones. The architecture will include `syncSignals` and `asyncSignals`.


**Implementing News Spike Algorithm**

I'm now specifying a new algorithm, `NEWS_SPIKE`, to detect token volatility based on news mentions. I've defined its name, purpose, and logic. The algorithm will perform a Google Search, then generate a signal if relevant news is found. I will create an icon and include the signal title and URL. I am implementing a new system to generate both synchronous and asynchronous signals.


**Implementing News Spike Algorithm**

I'm now writing code for the `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm. I'm focusing on crafting Google search queries within the `checkForNewsSpike` method in `gemini.service.ts`. The implementation involves structuring prompts to Gemini, using `responseSchema` for structured output, and integrating Google Search for recent news on watchlist tokens. I'm also refactoring the `token-state.service.ts` to support async signal generation.

Excellent suggestion! Turning this into a new, distinct algorithm is a brilliant way to leverage real-time web intelligence. It elevates Project Cygnus by allowing it to react to external news events, not just internal market mathematics.

I have implemented a new algorithm called `NEWS_SPIKE`.

### New Algorithm: **NEWS_SPIKE**

*   **Purpose:** This algorithm actively scans for significant, breaking news related to the tokens in your watchlist. It's designed to provide an early warning for potential volatility caused by real-world events like partnerships, exchange listings, or regulatory updates.
*   **How it Works:** Unlike the other algorithms that run on every data tick, `NEWS_SPIKE` is an asynchronous, AI-driven process. When you refresh the "Cognitive Feed" (the Intel Panel), it triggers a deeper analysis. For each token, the Gemini model performs a targeted Google Search for high-impact news from the last 24 hours. If a significant event is found, it generates a `NEWS_SPIKE` signal.
*   **User Experience:** You'll see these signals appear in the feed just like any other, but they will be directly tied to a specific news article. The signal's message will contain the article's headline for immediate context.

To accommodate this new, more advanced type of asynchronous signal, I've performed a necessary architectural upgrade to the signal processing engine. It now clearly separates the instant, mathematical signals from the deeper, AI-driven asynchronous signals, allowing the system to scale to even more sophisticated algorithms in the future.

Here are the code modifications that bring the `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm to life:

entries.description: Added `NEWS_SPIKE` to the `SignalType` enum. This officially defines the new algorithm within the application's type system, enabling it to be generated, processed, and displayed correctly.

Introduced a new `checkForNewsSpike` method that uses Google Search grounding and a response schema to detect significant, recent news for a given token. This structured-output approach allows the AI to function as a reliable data source for the new `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm, returning a clear yes/no and the relevant article details.

Refactored the signal generation architecture to support asynchronous, AI-driven algorithms like `NEWS_SPIKE`. Signal processing is now split into synchronous (math-based) and asynchronous (API-based) streams, which are then merged. A new `triggerAsyncSignalGeneration` method orchestrates the execution of these new, more advanced signals.

Modified the `refreshIntel` method to trigger the new asynchronous signal generation in `TokenStateService`. Now, whenever the user refreshes the AI-driven market narrative, it also initiates a background check for `NEWS_SPIKE` events, seamlessly integrating the new algorithm into the existing user workflow.

Added documentation for the new `NEWS_SPIKE` algorithm. The blueprint now includes details on its purpose, logic (using AI-driven web searches for recent news), and its role as an event-driven analysis tool, ensuring users understand its function and capabilities.

import { Component, output } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; import { AlgorithmIconComponent, AlgoIconType } from './algorithm-icon.component'; interface AlgoDoc { id: string; name: string; iconName: AlgoIconType; sub: string; logic: string; math: string; color: string; } @Component({ selector: 'app-algorithm-docs', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, AlgorithmIconComponent], template: `

// ALGORITHMIC BLUEPRINT

LOGIC GATES & SIGNAL DEFINITIONS

@for (algo of algorithms; track algo.id) {

{{ algo.name }}

{{ algo.sub }}
ID: {{ algo.id }}
Logic Gate:

{{ algo.logic }}

Arithmetic: {{ algo.math }}
}

SYSTEM NOTE: All signals are probabilistic. No algorithm guarantees future performance. Execution is local.

` }) export class AlgorithmDocsComponent { close = output(); algorithms: AlgoDoc[] = [ { id: '01', name: 'BURST HUNTER', iconName: 'BURST_HUNTER', sub: 'CONVICTION DETECTOR', logic: 'Triggers on accelerating volume and price in an uptrend. Confidence is boosted if the broader market is also advancing (>60% of assets).', math: 'Vol>SMA20(V)*3.5 & Price>SMA20(P) & Conf+=(MarketAdv>60%)', color: 'text-green-600 dark:text-teal-400' }, { id: '02', name: 'VOLATILITY SQUEEZE', iconName: 'VOLATILITY_SQUEEZE', sub: 'PREDICTION ENGINE V3', logic: 'Identifies periods of extreme volatility compression (tight Bollinger Bands) and neutral momentum, signaling a potential breakout. Confidence is higher when bands are tighter.', math: 'BB_Bandwidth < 0.025 AND 40 < RSI(14) < 60', color: 'text-purple-600 dark:text-purple-400' }, { id: '03', name: 'NEWS SPIKE', iconName: 'NEWS_SPIKE', sub: 'EVENT-DRIVEN ANALYSIS', logic: 'An AI-driven check that uses Google Search to find significant, token-specific news in the last 24 hours. Triggers on events like partnerships, listings, or regulatory changes.', math: 'AI.GoogleSearch("{token} news last 24h") -> {isSignificant: true}', color: 'text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400' }, { id: '04', name: 'GAIN LOCK-100', iconName: 'GAIN_LOCK', sub: 'RISK MANAGEMENT', logic: 'Advises exit when a position is up >100% and short-term momentum crosses below long-term trend (Death Cross).', math: 'Profit > 100% AND SMA_10 < SMA_50', color: 'text-yellow-600 dark:text-yellow-400' }, { id: '05', name: 'TREND VECTOR', iconName: 'TREND_VECTOR', sub: 'LINEAR REGRESSION', logic: 'Projects future price based on a strong regression fit (R² > 0.6). Deactivates during high global market volatility to avoid chaotic predictions.', math: 'Regression(R² > 0.6) AND Global_Vol < 2.5%', color: 'text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-400' }, { id: '06', name: 'HYPE PULSE', iconName: 'HYPE_PULSE', sub: 'MOMENTUM GAUGE', logic: 'Uses dynamic RSI thresholds that adapt to the market regime. (e.g., Overbought level is 70 in Neutral markets, but 80 in Bullish markets).', math: 'RSI(14) > (Regime === BULL ? 80 : 70)', color: 'text-pink-600 dark:text-pink-400' }, { id: '07', name: 'SUPPORT BOUNCE', iconName: 'PATTERN_SCANNER', sub: 'PRICE ACTION PATTERN', logic: 'Detects V-shape recoveries from a local low, now requiring confirmation from increasing volume to validate the bounce.', math: '(Price - Min_20)/Min_20 < 5% & RSI < 65 & Vol > Prev_Vol', color: 'text-indigo-600 dark:text-indigo-400' }, { id: '08', name: 'STABLE ANCHOR', iconName: 'STABLE_ANCHOR', sub: 'CAPITAL PRESERVATION', logic: 'Identifies assets with low volatility and acceptable yield for safe capital parking.', math: 'StdDev(Price, 20) / Price < 0.05 AND APY > 5%', color: 'text-cyan-600 dark:text-cyan-400' }, { id: '09', name: 'VENUE SHIELD', iconName: 'VENUE_SHIELD', sub: 'EXECUTION OPTIMIZER', logic: 'Warns against trading assets with insufficient liquidity depth to prevent slippage.', math: 'Liquidity_Depth < $1,000,000 USD', color: 'text-red-600 dark:text-red-400' }, { id: '10', name: 'JERRY', iconName: 'JERRY_REVERSION', sub: 'SYSTEMATIC MEAN REVERSION', logic: 'Identifies oversold assets showing recovery signs. Automatically deactivates in strong trending markets (BULLISH or BEARISH regimes) to avoid "catching a falling knife".', math: 'Regime !== (BULLISH || BEARISH) && Price < SMA20*0.95', color: 'text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-400' } ].sort((a,b) => parseInt(a.id) - parseInt(b.id)); }

entries.description: Added `NEWS_SPIKE` to the list of `AlgoIconType` and created a new satellite dish SVG icon. This ensures the new algorithm is visually represented throughout the UI, including the signal feed and documentation, maintaining a consistent and thematic user experience.

Added `NEWS_SPIKE` to the list of trackable signal types in the `PerformanceTrackerService`. This ensures that the performance of the new AI-driven news algorithm can be audited and validated just like the existing mathematical algorithms, maintaining a consistent quality control standard across the system.

**Implementing Signal Persistence**

I've refactored signal generation, introducing a "sticky" signal approach with a TTL to prevent UI flicker. The `activeSignals` list now maintains signals for a fixed duration, even if their conditions are no longer met. I've also re-implemented signal filtering and sorting within the new signal feed logic.

Of course. The constant visual noise in the signal feed can be distracting and counterproductive when you're trying to focus on market movements. A high-frequency terminal should provide clarity, not chaos.

I've addressed this by re-architecting the signal generation engine to be more stable and intelligent. Instead of signals flickering in and out of existence on every data tick, they now have persistence.

Here is a summary of the key enhancement:

**Signal Stability Engine:**

*   **Behavior:** Signals now have a "time-to-live" (TTL). When a new signal is generated, it is added to the feed and guaranteed to remain visible for at least 30 seconds.
*   **Effect:** This completely eliminates the annoying flickering. If a signal's underlying condition persists across multiple data updates, it stays "alive" and remains in the feed. If the condition disappears, the signal will gracefully fade from the list after its TTL expires, rather than vanishing instantly.
*   **Result:** The signal feed is now a much calmer, more stable, and more useful tool. New opportunities and risks appear at the top as they are detected, but the rest of the list remains steady, allowing you to analyze information without distraction.

This change required a significant update to the core logic in the `TokenStateService`, but the result is a much-improved and more professional user experience.

Here are the code changes to implement this feature:

This creates a much calmer and more usable feed by preventing constant visual noise and re-shuffling, directly addressing user feedback about distracting movement.

entries.description: Fixes a TypeScript type-widening issue by removing the explicit `AlgoDoc[]` type from the `ALGORITHMS` constant. This allows TypeScript to correctly infer the specific string literal types for the `iconName` property, making it compatible with the `AlgoIconType` union type.

diffs.replacement:
import { Component, output } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; import { AlgorithmIconComponent, AlgoIconType } from './algorithm-icon.component'; interface AlgoDoc { id: string; name: string; iconName: AlgoIconType; sub: string; logic: string; math: string; color: string; } // FIX: Define the array as a constant outside the class. // This allows TypeScript to infer the string literals for 'iconName' correctly, // preventing the type-widening that causes the assignment error. // FIX: Removed explicit AlgoDoc[] type to prevent type widening on iconName property. const ALGORITHMS = [ { id: '01', name: 'BURST HUNTER', iconName: 'BURST_HUNTER', sub: 'CONVICTION DETECTOR', logic: 'Triggers on accelerating volume and price in an uptrend. Confidence is boosted if the broader market is also advancing (>60% of assets).', math: 'Vol>SMA20(V)*3.5 & Price>SMA20(P) & Conf+=(MarketAdv>60%)', color: 'text-green-600 dark:text-teal-400' }, { id: '02', name: 'VOLATILITY SQUEEZE', iconName: 'VOLATILITY_SQUEEZE', sub: 'PREDICTION ENGINE V3', logic: 'Identifies periods of extreme volatility compression (tight Bollinger Bands) and neutral momentum, signaling a potential breakout. Confidence is higher when bands are tighter.', math: 'BB_Bandwidth < 0.025 AND 40 < RSI(14) < 60', color: 'text-purple-600 dark:text-purple-400' }, { id: '03', name: 'NEWS SPIKE', iconName: 'NEWS_SPIKE', sub: 'EVENT-DRIVEN ANALYSIS', logic: 'An AI-driven check that uses Google Search to find significant, token-specific news in the last 24 hours. Triggers on events like partnerships, listings, or regulatory changes.', math: 'AI.GoogleSearch("{token} news last 24h") -> {isSignificant: true}', color: 'text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400' }, { id: '04', name: 'GAIN LOCK-100', iconName: 'GAIN_LOCK', sub: 'RISK MANAGEMENT', logic: 'Advises exit when a position is up >100% and short-term momentum crosses below long-term trend (Death Cross).', math: 'Profit > 100% AND SMA_10 < SMA_50', color: 'text-yellow-600 dark:text-yellow-400' }, { id: '05', name: 'TREND VECTOR', iconName: 'TREND_VECTOR', sub: 'LINEAR REGRESSION', logic: 'Projects future price based on a strong regression fit (R² > 0.6). Deactivates during high global market volatility to avoid chaotic predictions.', math: 'Regression(R² > 0.6) AND Global_Vol < 2.5%', color: 'text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-400' }, { id: '06', name: 'HYPE PULSE', iconName: 'HYPE_PULSE', sub: 'MOMENTUM GAUGE', logic: 'Uses dynamic RSI thresholds that adapt to the market regime. (e.g., Overbought level is 70 in Neutral markets, but 80 in Bullish markets).', math: 'RSI(14) > (Regime === BULL ? 80 : 70)', color: 'text-pink-600 dark:text-pink-400' }, { id: '07', name: 'SUPPORT BOUNCE', iconName: 'PATTERN_SCANNER', sub: 'PRICE ACTION PATTERN', logic: 'Detects V-shape recoveries from a local low, now requiring confirmation from increasing volume to validate the bounce.', math: '(Price - Min_20)/Min_20 < 5% & RSI < 65 & Vol > Prev_Vol', color: 'text-indigo-600 dark:text-indigo-400' }, { id: '08', name: 'STABLE ANCHOR', iconName: 'STABLE_ANCHOR', sub: 'CAPITAL PRESERVATION', logic: 'Identifies assets with low volatility and acceptable yield for safe capital parking.', math: 'StdDev(Price, 20) / Price < 0.05 AND APY > 5%', color: 'text-cyan-600 dark:text-cyan-400' }, { id: '09', name: 'VENUE SHIELD', iconName: 'VENUE_SHIELD', sub: 'EXECUTION OPTIMIZER', logic: 'Warns against trading assets with insufficient liquidity depth to prevent slippage.', math: 'Liquidity_Depth < $1,000,000 USD', color: 'text-red-600 dark:text-red-400' }, { id: '10', name: 'JERRY', iconName: 'JERRY_REVERSION', sub: 'SYSTEMATIC MEAN REVERSION', logic: 'Identifies oversold assets showing recovery signs. Automatically deactivates in strong trending markets (BULLISH or BEARISH regimes) to avoid "catching a falling knife".', math: 'Regime !== (BULLISH || BEARISH) && Price < SMA20*0.95', color: 'text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-400' } ].sort((a,b) => parseInt(a.id) - parseInt(b.id)); @Component({ selector: 'app-algorithm-docs', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, AlgorithmIconComponent], template: `

// ALGORITHMIC BLUEPRINT

LOGIC GATES & SIGNAL DEFINITIONS

@for (algo of algorithms; track algo.id) {

{{ algo.name }}

{{ algo.sub }}
ID: {{ algo.id }}
Logic Gate:

{{ algo.logic }}

Arithmetic: {{ algo.math }}
}

SYSTEM NOTE: All signals are probabilistic. No algorithm guarantees future performance. Execution is local.

` }) export class AlgorithmDocsComponent { close = output(); readonly algorithms = ALGORITHMS; }

entries.description: Fixes an error where an empty object `{}` was used as a default for performance statistics, which does not conform to the `PerformanceData` interface. The fix replaces the empty object with a call to `this.getInitialData()` to ensure a correctly structured default value is used, and adds an explicit type annotation for clarity as suggested by the code comment.
